2 
‘138. Dishonour of cheque for insufficiency, etc., of funds in the account.—Where any cheque drawn 
by a person on an account maintained by him with a banker for payment of any amount of money to 
another person from out of that account for the discharge, in whole or in part, of any debt or other liability, is 
returned by the bank unpaid, either because of the amount of money standing to the credit of that account 
is insufficient to honour the cheque or that it exceeds the amount arranged to be paid from that account by 
an agreement made with that bank, such person shall be deemed to have committed an offence and shall, 
without prejudice to any other provision of this Act, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may 
extend to two years, or with fine which may extend to twice the amount of the cheque, or with both:
Provided that nothing contained in this section shall apply unless—
